case_title: NORMAN GONOR, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF A VS. CRAIGSLIST, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRAIGSLIST, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFEND ANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. C.C. 1747.08 ON ITS FACE DOES NOT APPLY TO ONLINE TRANSACTIONS. EVEN IF IT DID NOT, AN ANALYSIS UNDER CASE LAW, LEGISLATIVE INTENT AND PUBLIC POLICY SUGGEST THAT SUCH TRANSACTIONS ARE NOT, AND SHOULD NOT BE, ENCOMPASSED BY THE ACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2011 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRAIGSLIST, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Kathryn S. Diemer of Diemer Whitman & Cardosi, LLP (408.971.6270) for plaintiff and responding party; Eric A. Schreiber of Schreiber & Schreiber, Inc. (818.789.2577) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; Bobbie J. Wilson of Perkins Coie, LLP (344.7000) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is sustained without leave to amend. C.C. 1747.08 on its face does not apply to online transactions. Even if it did not, an analysis under case law, legislative intent and public policy suggest that such transactions are not, and should not be, encompassed by the Act. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
